name: FutureCorp Space Acquisition 1
business: FutureCorp Space Acquisition 1 is a blank check company incorporated on March 12, 2026 as a Cayman Islands exempted company and formed for the purpose of effecting a merger, amalgamation, share exchange, asset acquisition, share purchase, reorganization or similar business combination with one or more businesses or entities. It has not selected any business combination target and has no current operations. It intends to concentrate on companies in the global space economy and adjacent industries.

exposures.sentence: While we may pursue an initial business combination target in any industry or geographic region, we intend to concentrate on companies in the global space economy and adjacent industries, including space manufacturing and component supply chains, launch platforms, in-orbit services and habitats, in-orbit computing and manufacturing, space-based telecommunications and Earth observation, and defense-related activities.

While we may pursue an initial business combination target in any industry or geographic region, we intend to concentrate on companies in the global space economy and adjacent industries, including space manufacturing and component supply chains, launch platforms, in-orbit services and habitats, in-orbit computing and manufacturing, space-based telecommunications and Earth observation, and defense-related activities.

These include launch services and reusable vehicles; satellite hardware and subsystems; positioning, navigation and connectivity; space habitats and human presence infrastructure; space-related manufacturing and its supply chains; in-space commerce, logistics and servicing; earth observation, remote sensing and geospatial intelligence; space-based telecommunications; and defense, national security and dual-use space applications.

While we may pursue an initial business combination target in any industry or geographic region, we intend to concentrate on companies in the global space economy and adjacent industries, including space manufacturing and component supply chains, launch platforms, in-orbit services and habitats, in-orbit computing and manufacturing, space-based telecommunications and Earth observation, and defense-related activities.

While we may pursue an initial business combination target in any industry or geographic region, we intend to concentrate on companies in the global space economy and adjacent industries, including space manufacturing and component supply chains, launch platforms, in-orbit services and habitats, in-orbit computing and manufacturing, space-based telecommunications and Earth observation, and defense-related activities.
First, we believe the convergence of artificial intelligence and in-orbit data processing represents a significant new category of commercial opportunity.

In addition, private companies are pursuing novel commercial applications in space, including pharmaceutical and biotechnology research in microgravity, in-orbit manufacturing of specialty materials and in-orbit data centers for cloud computing and artificial intelligence workloads.

tamClaims.claim: Revenue in the commercial space sector grew to approximately $613 billion in 2024, up from approximately $450 billion in 2020.
tamClaims.sentence: According to the Space Foundation, revenue in the commercial space sector grew to approximately $613 billion in 2024, up from approximately $450 billion in 2020.

tamClaims.claim: Independent forecasts project further growth to over $1 trillion by the early 2030s.
tamClaims.sentence: Independent forecasts project further growth to over $1 trillion by the early 2030s.

tamClaims.claim: Governments spent approximately $135 billion on space in 2024, of which approximately $73 billion was directed toward defense.
tamClaims.sentence: According to the Space Foundation, governments spent approximately $135 billion on space in 2024, of which approximately $73 billion was directed toward defense.

tamClaims.claim: Cumulative private investment in space companies has reached approximately $291 billion since 2015, deployed across more than 1,830 unique companies.
tamClaims.sentence: According to Space Capital, cumulative private investment in space companies has reached approximately $291 billion since 2015, deployed across more than 1,830 unique companies.

tamClaims.claim: Annual private investment reached approximately $26 billion in 2024, representing a 30% year-over-year increase.
tamClaims.sentence: Annual private investment reached approximately $26 billion in 2024, representing a 30% year-over-year increase.

tamClaims.claim: As of April 2026, 15 privately held space companies have disclosed last-round valuations over $1 billion.
tamClaims.sentence: As of April 2026, 15 privately held space companies have disclosed last-round valuations over $1 billion.
